text:
A 26-year-old man has been arrested by police after he was injured in an explosion in a hotel room near Paris' main airport, with investigators later finding suspected bombmaking materials and equipment in the area.

A man has been arrested in Paris on suspicion of bomb-making and planning a violent act.

The 26-year-old was badly burned by an explosion in a hotel room in Paris near Charles de Gaulle Airport on Monday.

He was treated by emergency crews and a subsequent search of the room revealed suspected “products and materials intended for the manufacture of explosive devices,” the French National Anti-Terrorist Prosecutor's Office said.

The man is reported by French media to be of Russian-Ukrainian nationality from the Donbas region of eastern Ukraine, parts of which are currently occupied by Russian forces.

France’s anti-terrorism prosecutors’ office has opened an investigation into the incident.

The man was hospitalised before being questioned by specialist police, Le Monde reported.

It is understood he is suspected of participation in a terrorist conspiracy and bomb plot.

The news comes just weeks before the Paris 2024 Olympics get underway, with competition to kick off on July 26.

Millions of tourists are expected to flood the city, which has been preparing for the international event for several months.

The country’s terrorism threat level remains at its highest since it was activated in March this year.

Greater levels of security have been rolled out across all sites linked to the Olympic and Paralympic Games, as well as places of worship, transport and government buildings.

title: Man arrested after suspected terror-related explosion in a hotel room near Paris just weeks before Olympics begins | Sky News Australia
description: A man has been arrested in Paris on suspicion of bomb-making and planning a violent act.
